sections.paragraph:
ἀναστενάζω 1 aor. ἀνεστέναξα (s. στενάζω; Aeschyl., Choëph. 335; Hdt. 1, 863; PGM 4, 2492f; Sir 25:18; La 1:4; Sus 22 Theod.; JosAs; ApcMos 9) sigh deeply ἀναστενάξας τῷ πνεύματι αὐτοῦ λέγει he sighed deeply in his spirit (=to himself; cp. Mk 2:8) and said Mk 8:12 (cp. 2 Macc 6:30; JGibson, BT 38, ’87, 122–25). ἀνεστενάζων ὅτι ἐθριαμβεύετο ὑπὸ τῆς πόλεως distressed over being subject to ridicule by the city, as though he were being exhibited in a triumphal procession AcPl Ha 4, 12f (for distress in an apparently hopeless situation cp. Il. 10, 9 al.).—Error for ἀτενίζω or ἐνατενίζω GJs 3:1; 12:2 (s. deStrycker p. 298; s. also the sequence ἀνεστέναξε καὶ ἦν ἀτενίζουσα JosAs 8:8).—CBonner, HTR 20, 1927, 171–81.—DELG s.v. στένω.

aor. aor. = aorist

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

BT BT =Bible Translator—List 6

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

deStrycker deStrycker = ÉdeStrycker; s. GJs—List 1

HTR HTR = Harvard Theological Review—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 72
